title: Implement Stage 2 minimum task-sampling floor (N≥5 proactive review)
analysis:
Stage 2 (TaskKnowledgeSync) currently assembles a payload containing Stage 1's flagged items, the active task tree, and recently completed tasks. However, its instructions (both system prompt and payload 'Your Task' section) direct it to focus on: (1) flagged items from Stage 1, (2) completed tasks without knowledge, (3) AI-generated task consistency. When Stage 1 flags 0 items, the agent has no explicit directive to inspect active tasks — creating a blind spot for in-progress and blocked tasks.

The fix adds a proactive sampling mechanism: a helper function `_select_proactive_sample()` that selects the top-N tasks prioritized by status (in-progress > blocked > review > pending > done) then by ID descending (proxy for recency, since tasks lack updated_at). This sample is rendered as a dedicated `### Proactive Task Sample` section in the payload, with a corresponding instruction in both the system prompt's Guidelines section and the payload's 'Your Task' section.

The sampling floor is a class attribute `MIN_TASK_SAMPLE = 5` on TaskKnowledgeSync, matching the pattern of other stage attributes (remediation_mode, episode_limit). Proactive sampling is skipped in remediation mode (focused runs should not do general spot-checking). No config schema or harness changes are needed — the default is self-contained on the class.

steps.description: Write failing test `test_proactive_sample_section_present_in_payload`: assemble_payload with active tasks and 0 flagged items produces payload containing '### Proactive Task Sample' section header

Write failing test `test_proactive_sample_prioritizes_in_progress_and_blocked`: given tasks with mixed statuses (done, pending, in-progress, blocked), the proactive sample lists in-progress and blocked tasks first
Write failing test `test_proactive_sample_capped_at_min_task_sample`: given >5 eligible tasks, only 5 appear in the proactive sample section
Write failing test `test_proactive_sample_includes_all_when_fewer_than_floor`: given <5 total tasks, all appear in sample (no padding)

Add `_select_proactive_sample(all_tasks, n)` helper to task_knowledge_sync.py: sorts by STATUS_PRIORITY dict (in-progress=0, blocked=1, review=2, pending=3, done=4) then by ID descending, returns first N. Add `MIN_TASK_SAMPLE = 5` class attribute to TaskKnowledgeSync. Add '### Proactive Task Sample' section to assemble_payload using _select_proactive_sample and _format_tasks
Write failing test `test_proactive_sample_skipped_in_remediation_mode`: when stage.remediation_mode=True, payload does NOT contain '### Proactive Task Sample' section
Guard the proactive sample section with `if not self.remediation_mode` in assemble_payload
Write failing test `test_system_prompt_includes_proactive_spot_check_guideline`: STAGE2_SYSTEM_PROMPT contains instruction about proactive task sample review

Update STAGE2_SYSTEM_PROMPT in prompts/stage2.py: add bullet to Guidelines about always reviewing the Proactive Task Sample (check in-progress for completion knowledge, blocked for unblock conditions, done for missing knowledge capture)
Write failing test `test_payload_your_task_includes_proactive_step`: the 'Your Task' section in the payload includes an instruction step for proactive spot-checking of the sampled tasks

Add proactive spot-check step to the 'Your Task' instruction list in assemble_payload (between current steps 5 and 6), directing the agent to review the proactive sample regardless of Stage 1 findings
Write test `test_select_proactive_sample_uses_id_descending_as_recency_proxy`: given tasks with same status but different IDs, higher-ID tasks appear first in sample (proxy for most-recently-updated)
Write test `test_proactive_sample_empty_task_tree`: when taskmaster returns 0 tasks, proactive sample section shows 'No tasks.' (graceful handling)

design_decisions.decision: Use class attribute MIN_TASK_SAMPLE = 5 rather than config/schema change
design_decisions.rationale: Matches existing pattern (remediation_mode is a class attribute). Avoids touching config/schema.py which is outside task scope. The harness can override the attribute in the future if configurability is needed, just as it sets remediation_mode.

design_decisions.decision: Use task ID descending as recency proxy
design_decisions.rationale: Taskmaster tasks have no updated_at timestamp. Task IDs are monotonically increasing, so higher ID = more recently created. This is an imperfect but practical proxy for 'most recently updated'. The status-priority sort is the primary sort key, with ID-recency as tiebreaker.

design_decisions.decision: Status priority order: in-progress > blocked > review > pending > done
design_decisions.rationale: Directly addresses the bug: in-progress and blocked tasks are the primary blind spot. Review tasks may need attention too. Pending tasks are lower priority (not yet started). Done tasks are lowest (only relevant if knowledge capture is missing, which existing instructions already cover).

design_decisions.decision: Proactive sample is a separate payload section, not merged into Active Task Tree
design_decisions.rationale: A dedicated section with its own heading makes the agent's obligation explicit and visible. If merged into the existing task list, the agent has no clear directive to act on specific tasks. The separate section + matching instruction step creates a clear contract.

design_decisions.decision: Skip proactive sampling in remediation mode
design_decisions.rationale: Remediation runs are focused on addressing Stage 3 findings. Adding proactive spot-checking would dilute the focused intent and waste tokens. The remediation_note already says 'Do not perform general task-knowledge sync.'

design_decisions.decision: Helper function _select_proactive_sample is module-level (not a method)
design_decisions.rationale: Matches existing pattern: _format_report, _format_flagged, _format_tasks are all module-level helpers in task_knowledge_sync.py. Keeps the function easily testable in isolation.

reuse.how: Reuse to render the proactive sample section, same format as Active Task Tree and Recently Completed Tasks
New tests extend or sit alongside this class, reusing the mock_deps and watermark fixtures pattern
Same conditional pattern to skip proactive sample when remediation_mode=True
Reuse config/mock_deps fixture pattern for new test class
